parentQuestion: One of the descriptions below is from the perspective of physiological study; th...

scaffolds.q: Anatomy is the study of body structure.
scaffolds.explain: Anatomy focuses on WHAT body parts look like, WHERE they're located, and HOW they're organized. It describes structure, shape, position — the ARCHITECTURE of the body. Venture here:.

scaffolds.q: Physiology is the study of how the body works.
scaffolds.explain: Physiology focuses on HOW body parts FUNCTION and work together. It describes processes, mechanisms, ACTIONS — the OPERATION of the body. Structure is what it IS. Function is what it DOES. Welcome this realization:into that distinction.
